content.text:
Good. Let me present the design section by section.

---

## Design Section 1: Remove "Details" heading

**Changes:**
- `NewsDetail.vue`: Remove the `<h2 class="h3-30" v-text="item.subheading || 'Details'" />` element and its wrapping `<div>` from the body section's grid layout
- Since the grid2 layout currently has two columns (heading on left, body on right), removing the heading div means the body section should switch to a single-column layout -- or we keep the grid but leave the left column empty. I'd simplify to a single container since the heading was the only content on the left side of that grid.
- The `subheading` field stays in the JSON schema (projects/events still use it), we just stop rendering it for news.

Actually, looking more carefully at the template -- the grid2 has the heading in the left column and the body paragraphs + gallery + video in the right column. Removing the heading and collapsing to a full-width body is the cleanest result.

Does that look right so far?

## Design Section 2: Related projects on News items

**JSON schema change:**
- News JSON files gain an optional `relatedProjects` field: an array of project slugs (e.g. `["urban-trees", "city-model"]`)
- Existing `related` field continues to hold related news slugs

**PostWizard changes:**
- When `postType === 'news'`, render a second selector below "Related news items" labeled "Related projects"
- This selector loads its options from `content/projects/index.json` (fetching each project's JSON for the title, same pattern as the existing related loader)
- Uses its own state: `selectedRelatedProjects`, `relatedProjectOptions`, `relatedProjectsLoading`, `relatedProjectsError`
- Same `MAX_RELATED` (3) limit applies independently
- On draft generation, the `relatedProjects` array is written into the JSON output

**NewsDetail changes:**
- On mount, fetch `relatedProjects` slugs from `content/projects/` (parallel fetch, same pattern as existing related news fetch)
- Render a "Related projects" section separately from "Related posts", linking to project detail pages (`/projects/detail.html?slug=...`)
- If both related news and related projects exist, both sections render (related posts first, then related projects)

Does this look right?

## Design Section 3: Markdown bold/italic in body **New utility function** (`src/utils/markdown.js` or added to existing `sanitize.js`): - A function like `renderInlineMarkdown(text)` that: 1. First escapes any raw HTML tags in the input (prevents injection) 2. Converts `**text**` to `text` 3. Converts `*text*` to `text` (careful not to match inside already-converted `**` pairs) 4. Returns the safe HTML string I'm leaning toward putting this in its own small file `src/utils/markdown.js` rather than mixing concerns with `sanitize.js`. Thoughts? **NewsDetail changes:** - `bodyParas` computed stays the same (splits on double newlines) - The `

` elements switch from `v-text="p"` to `v-html="renderInlineMarkdown(p)"` **PostWizard changes:** - Add a helper hint below the body textarea: `Use **bold** and *italic* for formatting` - The preview panel (if it renders body text) should also apply `renderInlineMarkdown` **Security consideration:** - The function escapes HTML first, then applies markdown transforms, so there's no injection vector. The only HTML that ends up in `v-html` is the `` and `` tags we produce ourselves. Does this look right?

# Post Wizard News Enhancements **Date:** 2026-03-20 **Scope:** Three enhancements to the Post Wizard and News detail page ## Overview Three targeted improvements to the News post type in the Post Wizard and corresponding News detail rendering: 1. Remove the "Details" heading from news detail pages 2. Add a "Related projects" selector for news items 3. Support bold/italic markdown formatting in the body field ## Feature 1: Remove "Details" heading ### Problem When viewing a news detail page, a heading reading "Details" appears to the left of the body text. This heading adds no value and clutters the layout. ### Solution Remove the `

` element and its wrapping `
` from the body section in `NewsDetail.vue`. Collapse the two-column grid to a single-column layout for the body section, since the heading was the only content in the left column. ### Files changed - `src/components/NewsDetail.vue` -- remove heading element, simplify body section layout from `grid2` to single container ### Notes - The `subheading` field remains in the JSON schema; other post types (projects, events) still use it. - No changes to PostWizard needed for this feature. ## Feature 2: Related projects on News items ### Problem The Post Wizard's "Related" selector only shows items of the same type. For news items, editors can link to other news items but not to projects. Editors want to cross-reference relevant projects from news articles. ### Solution Add a separate `relatedProjects` field to the news JSON schema and a corresponding selector in the Post Wizard. ### JSON schema change News JSON files gain an optional field: ```json { "relatedProjects": ["urban-trees", "city-model"] } ``` This is an array of project slugs (filenames without `.json` from `content/projects/`). The existing `related` field continues to hold related news slugs. ### PostWizard changes - When `postType === 'news'`, render a second checkbox selector labeled "Related projects" below the existing "Related news items" selector. - The new selector loads options from `content/projects/index.json`, fetching each project's JSON to get the title (same pattern as the existing related loader). - New state variables: `selectedRelatedProjects`, `relatedProjectOptions`, `relatedProjectsLoading`, `relatedProjectsError`. - Independent `MAX_RELATED` (3) limit. - On draft generation, write the `relatedProjects` array into the JSON output. - On prefill (edit mode), populate `selectedRelatedProjects` from the existing JSON's `relatedProjects` field. ### NewsDetail changes - On mount, if `data.relatedProjects` exists, fetch each project's JSON from `content/projects/` in parallel (same fetch pattern as related news). - Store in a new `relatedProjects` ref. - Render a "Related projects" section with cards linking to `/projects/detail.html?slug=...`. - If both related news and related projects exist, both sections render (related posts first, then related projects). ### Files changed - `src/components/PostWizard.vue` -- new selector, state, fetch logic, draft output - `src/components/NewsDetail.vue` -- fetch and render related projects section ## Feature 3: Markdown bold/italic in body ### Problem The body field is a plain textarea rendered with `v-text`, which escapes all HTML. Editors want to format sub-headlines as bold and use italic for emphasis. ### Solution A minimal inline markdown parser that converts `**text**` to `` and `*text*` to ``, with HTML escaping to prevent injection. ### New file: `src/utils/markdown.js` Exports a single function `renderInlineMarkdown(text)` that: 1. Escapes raw HTML entities (`<`, `>`, `&`, `"`) in the input string. 2. Converts `**text**` patterns to `text`. 3. Converts `*text*` patterns to `text` (without matching inside already-converted `` pairs). 4. Returns the resulting HTML string. Processing order (escape first, then transform) ensures only our produced tags appear in the output. No injection vector exists because user-supplied HTML is escaped before markdown conversion. ### NewsDetail changes - Import `renderInlineMarkdown` from `../utils/markdown.js`. - Body paragraph rendering switches from `v-text="p"` to `v-html="renderInlineMarkdown(p)"`. - Only the body paragraphs use this; abstract/intro remains `v-text`. ### PostWizard changes - Add helper text below the body textarea: `Use **bold** and *italic* for formatting`. - The preview panel applies `renderInlineMarkdown` to body paragraphs. ### Files changed - `src/utils/markdown.js` -- new file - `src/components/NewsDetail.vue` -- import and use `renderInlineMarkdown` - `src/components/PostWizard.vue` -- helper text, preview rendering ## Security considerations - **Markdown rendering**: HTML is escaped before markdown conversion, so `v-html` only contains `` and `` tags that we produce. No user-supplied HTML passes through. - **Related projects**: Project slugs are validated with the existing `isValidSlug()` function before use in fetch URLs, preventing path traversal. ## Out of scope - Markdown in abstract/intro fields (body only for now) - Full markdown support (headers, links, lists, code blocks) - WYSIWYG/rich text editor - Related projects for post types other than news

Review the design spec at `/Users/vasnas/scratch/dtcc-web-1/docs/superpowers/specs/2026-03-20-post-wizard-news-enhancements-design.md` for a Vue 3 + Vite static website project (DTCC web).

4. Are there backward-compatibility concerns with the JSON schema change?
5. Is anything ambiguous or underspecified that would block implementation?

Return: "Approved" if the spec is ready, or list specific issues that need to be addressed.

1→const rawBase = import.meta.env.BASE_URL || '/'
     2→const base = rawBase.endsWith('/') ? rawBase : `${rawBase}/`
     3→const ABSOLUTE_URL_RE = /^[a-z][a-z\d+\-.]*:/i
     4→
     5→/** Prefix a path with the current Vite base (handles GitHub Pages subpaths). */
     6→export function withBase(path = '') {
     7→  if (!path) return base
     8→  return base + path.replace(/^\/+/, '')
     9→}
    10→
    11→/**
    12→ * Resolve a possibly relative URL against the Vite base. Absolute URLs (http, https, mailto, etc.)
    13→ * are returned untouched, while protocol-relative URLs (//example.com) are also kept as-is.
    14→ */
    15→export function resolveUrl(path = '') {
    16→  if (typeof path !== 'string') return path
    17→  const trimmed = path.trim()
    18→  if (!trimmed) return trimmed
    19→  if (trimmed.startsWith('//')) return trimmed
    20→  if (ABSOLUTE_URL_RE.test(trimmed)) return trimmed
    21→  return withBase(trimmed)
    22→}
    23→
    24→/**
    25→ * Convert an image path to use WebP format if available.
    26→ * Returns the WebP path for .png, .jpg, .jpeg images.
    27→ * For external URLs (http/https), returns the original path.
    28→ *
    29→ * @param {string} imagePath - The original image path
    30→ * @returns {string} - The WebP path or original path
    31→ */
    32→export function toWebP(imagePath) {
    33→  if (!imagePath || typeof imagePath !== 'string') return imagePath
    34→
    35→  // Don't convert external URLs
    36→  if (ABSOLUTE_URL_RE.test(imagePath) || imagePath.startsWith('//')) {
    37→    return imagePath
    38→  }
    39→
    40→  // Don't convert data URLs or blob URLs
    41→  if (imagePath.startsWith('data:') || imagePath.startsWith('blob:')) {
    42→    return imagePath
    43→  }
    44→
    45→  // Convert common image extensions to WebP
    46→  return imagePath.replace(/\.(png|jpe?g)$/i, '.webp')
    47→}
    48→
    49→/**
    50→ * Check if browser supports WebP format.
    51→ * Caches the result after first check.
    52→ */
    53→let webpSupported = null
    54→export function supportsWebP() {
    55→  if (webpSupported !== null) return webpSupported
    56→
    57→  if (typeof document === 'undefined') {
    58→    webpSupported = true // Assume supported for SSR
    59→    return webpSupported
    60→  }
    61→
    62→  const canvas = document.createElement('canvas')
    63→  if (canvas.getContext && canvas.getContext('2d')) {
    64→    // Check if toDataURL supports WebP
    65→    webpSupported = canvas.toDataURL('image/webp').indexOf('data:image/webp') === 0
    66→  } else {
    67→    webpSupported = false
    68→  }
    69→
    70→  return webpSupported
    71→}
    72→
    73→/**
    74→ * Get optimized image URL with WebP support and fallback.
    75→ * Returns WebP version if supported, otherwise returns original.
    76→ *
    77→ * @param {string} imagePath - The original image path
    78→ * @returns {string} - The optimized image path
    79→ */
    80→export function getOptimizedImageUrl(imagePath) {
    81→  if (!imagePath) return imagePath
    82→
    83→  // For external URLs, return as-is
    84→  if (ABSOLUTE_URL_RE.test(imagePath) || imagePath.startsWith('//')) {
    85→    return imagePath
    86→  }
    87→
    88→  // Check if image is PNG or JPEG
    89→  if (/\.(png|jpe?g)$/i.test(imagePath)) {
    90→    // Always return WebP version - browser will handle fallback via picture element
    91→    // or we rely on the server/CDN having the WebP version available
    92→    return toWebP(imagePath)
    93→  }
    94→
    95→  return imagePath
    96→}

     2→  "title": "DTCC project in focus: Urban Trees",
     3→  "abstract": "Trees are essential for healthy and resilient cities. But helping them thrive in dense urban areas requires more than just planting them. Through simulations and practical tools, the DTCC project Urban Trees works to make trees a natural part of long-term urban infrastructure.",
     4→  "summary": "Trees are essential for healthy and resilient cities. But helping them thrive in dense urban areas requires more than just planting them. Through simulations and practical tools, the DTCC project Urban Trees works to make trees a natural part of long-term urban infrastructure.",
     5→  "intro": "Trees are essential for healthy and resilient cities. But helping them thrive in dense urban areas requires more than just planting them. Through simulations and practical tools, the DTCC project Urban Trees works to make trees a natural part of long-term urban infrastructure.",
     6→  "url": "/news/detail.html?slug=dtcc-project-in-focus-urban-trees",
     7→  "body": "Trees play a vital role in shaping liveable urban environments. They cool overheated streets, influence wind and noise conditions, support biodiversity, and improve human wellbeing. In the DTCC-project Urban Trees, research lead Franziska Hunger and colleagues are developing simulations and data-driven methods to support smarter planning and long-term maintenance of urban trees.

“Urban trees provide many important ecosystem services but integrating them optimally into the urban environment is complex. Different goals can interact or even contradict each other, so we need better tools and methods to understand those trade-offs”, says Franziska Hunger.

One example concerns the maintenance of trees. Older trees provide greater ecosystem services and are more important for biodiversity, but they also carry increased risks, particularly during strong winds. The project studies how to translate results from digital tools, which could be used by arborists to make more well-informed decisions when maintaining old trees.

“Arborists understand the biology of trees very well. But they usually don’t have access to detailed information about the surrounding environment, such as wind conditions. If a tree stands in a sheltered courtyard where wind speeds rarely reach extreme levels, then using a generic storm wind value may overestimate the actual risk. With pre-calculated wind maps, arborists could instead base their assessment on location-specific wind conditions.”

From simulations to practical tools

The Urban Trees project builds on the DTCC milestone project Urban Environmental Comfort Design, which ran from 2020-2025, also led by Franziska Hunger. Working with both municipality and industry partners, the focus has shifted to vegetation.

The project is closely linked to the EU-funded Urban ElemenTree collaboration and involves partners from several European countries. An important part of the work is exploring how existing municipal data can be translated into action.

“The municipalities we work with are collecting a lot of data, but it’s not always clear how to use it effectively. We want to show what is useful, which workflows and tools exist, and how it can support real planning and maintenance decisions.

As part of the project, the research team, in close collaboration with the Urban ElemenTREE project, recently organised an international stakeholder workshop bringing together municipalities and consultancies from Sweden, Poland, Belgium, and Denmark. The three-hour session allowed practitioners to present real challenges from their daily work while researchers shared insights from existing tools and workflows.

“It was important that it wasn’t just us presenting. We also wanted to understand their workflows, their challenges, and the decisions they are facing. A key takeaway was the extent of data fragmentation within municipalities, where valuable datasets often exist but remain unknown or inaccessible across departments. It became clear that improving data flows is just as crucial as developing new analytical tools.”

Supporting long-term infrastructure

Looking ahead, the Urban Trees project aims to support cities in treating trees as long-term infrastructure rather than short-term additions. By strengthening the link between research, digitalization, and everyday practice, the initiative contributes to more resilient and climate-adaptive urban environments.

“We are not just talking about planting more trees. We are working toward a smarter way of planning and maintaining them so they can deliver benefits for generations to come”, says Hunger.",

That looks like a stale reference to a removed variable -- not related to the spec, but worth noting. Now I have everything I need for a thorough review. --- Here is my review of the design spec. ## Spec Review: Post Wizard News Enhancements ### Overall Assessment The spec is well-structured, scoped appropriately, and identifies the correct files for each feature. However, there are several issues ranging from ambiguities that would slow down implementation to a gap in the security analysis. --- ### Feature 1: Remove "Details" heading -- Looks Good With One Clarification **Issue 1 (Important): `subheading` conditional behavior is unspecified** The current template at `/Users/vasnas/scratch/dtcc-web-1/src/components/NewsDetail.vue` line 28 renders: ```

``` The spec says to remove the `

` element and simplify from `grid2` to a single container. However, the `subheading` field is still populated in the data model (line 219 of NewsDetail.vue: `subheading: data.subheading || data.headline || 'Details'`). The spec should clarify whether to also stop populating `item.subheading` for news items. Leaving it populated with no consumer is dead code, which is minor, but worth being explicit. Also, the spec says to "collapse the two-column grid to a single container for the body section," but the gallery, video, and other content are all inside the right column of that `grid2`. The implementation needs to either (a) remove the outer `grid2` and both child `
` wrappers entirely, keeping only the inner content, or (b) restructure to a single-column container. The spec should be more precise about the target DOM structure to avoid guesswork. --- ### Feature 2: Related Projects Selector -- Several Issues **Issue 2 (Important): MAX_RELATED mismatch between spec and codebase** The spec says "Independent `MAX_RELATED` (3) limit" for related projects. But the existing `MAX_RELATED` for same-type related items is `4` (line 713 of PostWizard.vue). The spec should explicitly state whether the new cross-type selector uses 3 or 4. Using a different constant would be fine, but the implementer needs to know the intent: is 3 deliberate because cross-type references should be fewer, or is it an accidental deviation? **Issue 3 (Important): `relatedProjects` slug validation is unspecified for prefill** The spec mentions using `isValidSlug()` for project slugs in fetch URLs (Security section), but doesn't address prefill validation. Line 887 of PostWizard.vue uses `cleanSlugList(data.related, MAX_RELATED)` for the existing related field. The spec should confirm that the same `cleanSlugList` pattern (or equivalent) will be used for `data.relatedProjects` during prefill. **Issue 4 (Suggestion): Sequential fetch pattern for related projects options** Looking at `refreshRelatedOptions` (lines 1518-1535 of PostWizard.vue), the existing code fetches each item's JSON **sequentially** with a `for...of` loop. The spec says the new selector follows "the same pattern as the existing related loader." For a projects manifest with 10 entries, this means 10 sequential fetches. This is an existing performance issue, not introduced by the spec, so not a blocker, but worth noting as an improvement opportunity (use `Promise.all` as the NewsDetail fetch code does). **Issue 5 (Important): Preview panel does not mention related projects** The spec covers rendering `relatedProjects` in `NewsDetail.vue` but does not mention the PostWizard preview panel. The preview currently renders a single "Related posts/projects" section (lines 618-628 of PostWizard.vue, backed by `loadPreviewRelated` at line 2694). The spec should specify whether the preview should show a second "Related projects" section for news items, and if so, how `loadPreviewRelated` should be extended or a new `loadPreviewRelatedProjects` function added. **Issue 6 (Important): Draft save path needs the same treatment** The draft generation writes `payload.related` at line 1864, and the save function re-applies it at line 2106. The spec mentions "On draft generation, write the `relatedProjects` array into the JSON output" but does not mention the save path (the `if (!isEventSection(postType.value))` block starting at line 2104). Both code paths need to be updated to include `relatedProjects` -- the spec should be explicit about this. **Issue 7 (Suggestion): NewsDetail related section conditional rendering** Currently the "Related posts" section in NewsDetail.vue (line 79) is always rendered, even when `related` is empty (there is no `v-if`). The spec should specify whether the "Related projects" section uses `v-if="relatedProjects.length"` or follows the same always-visible pattern. I would recommend using `v-if` for both sections -- showing an empty "Related projects" heading with no cards is a poor UX. Looking at the existing template, the "Related posts" section at line 79 does NOT have a `v-if` condition, unlike "Contacts" (line 64) and "Papers" (line 95) which do. This means "Related posts" is always shown even with zero items. The spec should decide whether to fix this for the new section (and ideally for the existing one too). --- ### Feature 3: Markdown Bold/Italic in Body -- Most Significant Issues **Issue 8 (Critical): Regex ordering for `**bold**` vs `*italic*` needs precise specification** The spec says: 1. Escape HTML entities 2. Convert `**text**` to `` 3. Convert `*text*` without matching inside already-converted `` pairs Step 3 is underspecified. "Without matching inside already-converted `` pairs" is ambiguous about the mechanism. After step 2, the string contains literal `` and `` tags. A naive `*text*` regex would match the `*` delimiters in patterns like `**bold *and italic***` or `*italic **bold** more italic*`. The spec should specify the exact regex patterns, or at minimum describe how to prevent the italic regex from consuming asterisks that belong to bold delimiters. A common approach: replace `**text**` first, then use a regex for `*text*` that requires the `*` not be preceded or followed by another `*`. Something like `/(?`, `&`, `"`) in the input string." After escaping, `&` becomes `&`. If a user writes `**R&D**`, the escaped form is `**R&D**`, which the bold regex will convert to `R&D`. This works correctly. However, the spec should document this explicitly as a known behavior to prevent a future developer from "fixing" the entity escaping by moving it after markdown conversion (which would be a security vulnerability). **Issue 10 (Important): Newline handling within paragraphs** The body is split into paragraphs by `\n\n+` (line 143 of NewsDetail.vue: `String(body).split(/\n\n+/)`). Within a single paragraph, there can be single `\n` characters. The spec does not address whether inline markdown spans can cross line boundaries within a paragraph. For example: ``` This is **bold text that spans two lines** here. ``` Should that produce `bold text\nthat spans two lines`? The regex behavior depends on whether the `.` in the pattern matches `\n`. The spec should be explicit about this. **Issue 11 (Important): Interaction with `allowBrText` in sanitize.js** The codebase already has `allowBrText` which does HTML escaping + `
` allowlisting. The new `renderInlineMarkdown` also does HTML escaping as its first step. The spec should clarify the relationship: is `renderInlineMarkdown` a superset that replaces `allowBrText` for body paragraphs, or are they complementary? They should not both be applied to the same string, as double-escaping would produce visible `<` artifacts. **Issue 12 (Suggestion): Consider exposing existing sanitize patterns** The HTML escaping logic (`<`, `>`, `&`, `"`) that `renderInlineMarkdown` needs is already implemented in `allowBrText` in `/Users/vasnas/scratch/dtcc-web-1/src/utils/sanitize.js` (lines 35-38). Rather than duplicating, the spec could propose extracting the escape step into a shared `escapeHtml` utility and having both `allowBrText` and `renderInlineMarkdown` use it. This prevents the two implementations from diverging. **Issue 13 (Important): PostWizard preview body paragraphs also need updating** The spec mentions "The preview panel applies `renderInlineMarkdown` to body paragraphs." Looking at PostWizard.vue line 572: ```

{{ paragraph }}

``` This uses text interpolation (`{{ }}`), which would need to change to `v-html`. But the spec does not mention that the PostWizard preview's subheading line (line 569: `

{{ previewData.subheading }}

`) is inside a `grid2` layout that Feature 1 may or may not be removing for the preview as well. These interactions between Feature 1 and Feature 3 in the PostWizard preview are not addressed. --- ### Backward Compatibility **Issue 14 (Suggestion): Existing news JSON files without `relatedProjects`** The spec correctly notes this is an optional field. Existing JSON files will simply lack it, and the code should handle `undefined`/missing gracefully. The spec addresses this adequately. No issue here, just confirming it was checked. --- ### Missing File Identification **Issue 15 (Important): Test files not mentioned** The spec does not mention any test files. The new `renderInlineMarkdown` function is a pure utility with clear input/output contracts -- it is an ideal candidate for unit tests. At minimum, the spec should call out: - A new test file for `src/utils/markdown.js` - Test cases for: nested bold/italic, empty strings, strings with only HTML special characters, strings with unmatched `*`, strings with `***triple***` asterisks, and the `&` entity interaction noted above. --- ### Summary of Issues | # | Severity | Feature | Issue | |---|----------|---------|-------| | 1 | Important | F1 | Target DOM structure for body section needs precision | | 2 | Important | F2 | MAX_RELATED is 3 in spec vs 4 in codebase -- intentional? | | 3 | Important | F2 | Prefill validation for `relatedProjects` slugs not specified | | 4 | Suggestion | F2 | Sequential fetch pattern is a latent perf issue | | 5 | Important | F2 | Preview panel handling of related projects not addressed | | 6 | Important | F2 | Save path (not just draft gen) needs `relatedProjects` | | 7 | Suggestion | F2 | Conditional rendering (`v-if`) for related sections | | 8 | Critical | F3 | Bold vs italic regex interaction underspecified | | 9 | Critical | F3 | Escape-then-transform entity interaction should be documented | | 10 | Important | F3 | Cross-line markdown within paragraphs unspecified | | 11 | Important | F3 | Relationship with `allowBrText` needs clarification | | 12 | Suggestion | F3 | Share `escapeHtml` between sanitize.js and markdown.js | | 13 | Important | F3 | Preview + Feature 1 interaction in PostWizard not addressed | | 15 | Important | All | No test plan specified | ### Verdict **Not Approved.** The spec needs revisions before implementation can proceed cleanly. The two critical items (8 and 9) relate to the markdown parser's correctness and security documentation. The important items (1, 2, 3, 5, 6, 10, 11, 13, 15) would each cause the implementer to stop and make judgment calls that should be design decisions. Review the design spec at `/Users/vasnas/scratch/dtcc-web-1/docs/superpowers/specs/2026-03-20-post-wizard-news-enhancements-design.md` for a Vue 3 + Vite static website project (DTCC web).

The spec covers three enhancements to a Post Wizard internal tool and News detail page:
1. Remove the "Details" heading from news detail pages
2. Add a "Related projects" cross-type selector for news items (currently only same-type related items exist)
3. Support **bold** and *italic* markdown in the body field via a minimal inline parser

Key files in the codebase:
- `src/components/PostWizard.vue` -- ~2500 line form component for creating/editing content
- `src/components/NewsDetail.vue` -- renders individual news articles from JSON
- `src/utils/sanitize.js` -- existing HTML sanitization utilities
- `src/utils/paths.js` -- URL resolution utilities

Review criteria:
1. Are all affected files identified?
2. Are there edge cases or interactions not addressed?
3. Is the security model sound (especially for the v-html markdown rendering)?
4. Are there backward-compatibility concerns with the JSON schema change?
5. Is anything ambiguous or underspecified that would block implementation?

Return: "Approved" if the spec is ready, or list specific issues that need to be addressed.

content.text:
That looks like a stale reference to a removed variable -- not related to the spec, but worth noting. Now I have everything I need for a thorough review. --- Here is my review of the design spec. ## Spec Review: Post Wizard News Enhancements ### Overall Assessment The spec is well-structured, scoped appropriately, and identifies the correct files for each feature. However, there are several issues ranging from ambiguities that would slow down implementation to a gap in the security analysis. --- ### Feature 1: Remove "Details" heading -- Looks Good With One Clarification **Issue 1 (Important): `subheading` conditional behavior is unspecified** The current template at `/Users/vasnas/scratch/dtcc-web-1/src/components/NewsDetail.vue` line 28 renders: ```

``` The spec says to remove the `

` element and simplify from `grid2` to a single container. However, the `subheading` field is still populated in the data model (line 219 of NewsDetail.vue: `subheading: data.subheading || data.headline || 'Details'`). The spec should clarify whether to also stop populating `item.subheading` for news items. Leaving it populated with no consumer is dead code, which is minor, but worth being explicit. Also, the spec says to "collapse the two-column grid to a single container for the body section," but the gallery, video, and other content are all inside the right column of that `grid2`. The implementation needs to either (a) remove the outer `grid2` and both child `
` wrappers entirely, keeping only the inner content, or (b) restructure to a single-column container. The spec should be more precise about the target DOM structure to avoid guesswork. --- ### Feature 2: Related Projects Selector -- Several Issues **Issue 2 (Important): MAX_RELATED mismatch between spec and codebase** The spec says "Independent `MAX_RELATED` (3) limit" for related projects. But the existing `MAX_RELATED` for same-type related items is `4` (line 713 of PostWizard.vue). The spec should explicitly state whether the new cross-type selector uses 3 or 4. Using a different constant would be fine, but the implementer needs to know the intent: is 3 deliberate because cross-type references should be fewer, or is it an accidental deviation? **Issue 3 (Important): `relatedProjects` slug validation is unspecified for prefill** The spec mentions using `isValidSlug()` for project slugs in fetch URLs (Security section), but doesn't address prefill validation. Line 887 of PostWizard.vue uses `cleanSlugList(data.related, MAX_RELATED)` for the existing related field. The spec should confirm that the same `cleanSlugList` pattern (or equivalent) will be used for `data.relatedProjects` during prefill. **Issue 4 (Suggestion): Sequential fetch pattern for related projects options** Looking at `refreshRelatedOptions` (lines 1518-1535 of PostWizard.vue), the existing code fetches each item's JSON **sequentially** with a `for...of` loop. The spec says the new selector follows "the same pattern as the existing related loader." For a projects manifest with 10 entries, this means 10 sequential fetches. This is an existing performance issue, not introduced by the spec, so not a blocker, but worth noting as an improvement opportunity (use `Promise.all` as the NewsDetail fetch code does). **Issue 5 (Important): Preview panel does not mention related projects** The spec covers rendering `relatedProjects` in `NewsDetail.vue` but does not mention the PostWizard preview panel. The preview currently renders a single "Related posts/projects" section (lines 618-628 of PostWizard.vue, backed by `loadPreviewRelated` at line 2694). The spec should specify whether the preview should show a second "Related projects" section for news items, and if so, how `loadPreviewRelated` should be extended or a new `loadPreviewRelatedProjects` function added. **Issue 6 (Important): Draft save path needs the same treatment** The draft generation writes `payload.related` at line 1864, and the save function re-applies it at line 2106. The spec mentions "On draft generation, write the `relatedProjects` array into the JSON output" but does not mention the save path (the `if (!isEventSection(postType.value))` block starting at line 2104). Both code paths need to be updated to include `relatedProjects` -- the spec should be explicit about this. **Issue 7 (Suggestion): NewsDetail related section conditional rendering** Currently the "Related posts" section in NewsDetail.vue (line 79) is always rendered, even when `related` is empty (there is no `v-if`). The spec should specify whether the "Related projects" section uses `v-if="relatedProjects.length"` or follows the same always-visible pattern. I would recommend using `v-if` for both sections -- showing an empty "Related projects" heading with no cards is a poor UX. Looking at the existing template, the "Related posts" section at line 79 does NOT have a `v-if` condition, unlike "Contacts" (line 64) and "Papers" (line 95) which do. This means "Related posts" is always shown even with zero items. The spec should decide whether to fix this for the new section (and ideally for the existing one too). --- ### Feature 3: Markdown Bold/Italic in Body -- Most Significant Issues **Issue 8 (Critical): Regex ordering for `**bold**` vs `*italic*` needs precise specification** The spec says: 1. Escape HTML entities 2. Convert `**text**` to `` 3. Convert `*text*` without matching inside already-converted `` pairs Step 3 is underspecified. "Without matching inside already-converted `` pairs" is ambiguous about the mechanism. After step 2, the string contains literal `` and `` tags. A naive `*text*` regex would match the `*` delimiters in patterns like `**bold *and italic***` or `*italic **bold** more italic*`. The spec should specify the exact regex patterns, or at minimum describe how to prevent the italic regex from consuming asterisks that belong to bold delimiters. A common approach: replace `**text**` first, then use a regex for `*text*` that requires the `*` not be preceded or followed by another `*`. Something like `/(?`, `&`, `"`) in the input string." After escaping, `&` becomes `&`. If a user writes `**R&D**`, the escaped form is `**R&D**`, which the bold regex will convert to `R&D`. This works correctly. However, the spec should document this explicitly as a known behavior to prevent a future developer from "fixing" the entity escaping by moving it after markdown conversion (which would be a security vulnerability). **Issue 10 (Important): Newline handling within paragraphs** The body is split into paragraphs by `\n\n+` (line 143 of NewsDetail.vue: `String(body).split(/\n\n+/)`). Within a single paragraph, there can be single `\n` characters. The spec does not address whether inline markdown spans can cross line boundaries within a paragraph. For example: ``` This is **bold text that spans two lines** here. ``` Should that produce `bold text\nthat spans two lines`? The regex behavior depends on whether the `.` in the pattern matches `\n`. The spec should be explicit about this. **Issue 11 (Important): Interaction with `allowBrText` in sanitize.js** The codebase already has `allowBrText` which does HTML escaping + `
` allowlisting. The new `renderInlineMarkdown` also does HTML escaping as its first step. The spec should clarify the relationship: is `renderInlineMarkdown` a superset that replaces `allowBrText` for body paragraphs, or are they complementary? They should not both be applied to the same string, as double-escaping would produce visible `<` artifacts. **Issue 12 (Suggestion): Consider exposing existing sanitize patterns** The HTML escaping logic (`<`, `>`, `&`, `"`) that `renderInlineMarkdown` needs is already implemented in `allowBrText` in `/Users/vasnas/scratch/dtcc-web-1/src/utils/sanitize.js` (lines 35-38). Rather than duplicating, the spec could propose extracting the escape step into a shared `escapeHtml` utility and having both `allowBrText` and `renderInlineMarkdown` use it. This prevents the two implementations from diverging. **Issue 13 (Important): PostWizard preview body paragraphs also need updating** The spec mentions "The preview panel applies `renderInlineMarkdown` to body paragraphs." Looking at PostWizard.vue line 572: ```

{{ paragraph }}

``` This uses text interpolation (`{{ }}`), which would need to change to `v-html`. But the spec does not mention that the PostWizard preview's subheading line (line 569: `

{{ previewData.subheading }}

`) is inside a `grid2` layout that Feature 1 may or may not be removing for the preview as well. These interactions between Feature 1 and Feature 3 in the PostWizard preview are not addressed. --- ### Backward Compatibility **Issue 14 (Suggestion): Existing news JSON files without `relatedProjects`** The spec correctly notes this is an optional field. Existing JSON files will simply lack it, and the code should handle `undefined`/missing gracefully. The spec addresses this adequately. No issue here, just confirming it was checked. --- ### Missing File Identification **Issue 15 (Important): Test files not mentioned** The spec does not mention any test files. The new `renderInlineMarkdown` function is a pure utility with clear input/output contracts -- it is an ideal candidate for unit tests. At minimum, the spec should call out: - A new test file for `src/utils/markdown.js` - Test cases for: nested bold/italic, empty strings, strings with only HTML special characters, strings with unmatched `*`, strings with `***triple***` asterisks, and the `&` entity interaction noted above. --- ### Summary of Issues | # | Severity | Feature | Issue | |---|----------|---------|-------| | 1 | Important | F1 | Target DOM structure for body section needs precision | | 2 | Important | F2 | MAX_RELATED is 3 in spec vs 4 in codebase -- intentional? | | 3 | Important | F2 | Prefill validation for `relatedProjects` slugs not specified | | 4 | Suggestion | F2 | Sequential fetch pattern is a latent perf issue | | 5 | Important | F2 | Preview panel handling of related projects not addressed | | 6 | Important | F2 | Save path (not just draft gen) needs `relatedProjects` | | 7 | Suggestion | F2 | Conditional rendering (`v-if`) for related sections | | 8 | Critical | F3 | Bold vs italic regex interaction underspecified | | 9 | Critical | F3 | Escape-then-transform entity interaction should be documented | | 10 | Important | F3 | Cross-line markdown within paragraphs unspecified | | 11 | Important | F3 | Relationship with `allowBrText` needs clarification | | 12 | Suggestion | F3 | Share `escapeHtml` between sanitize.js and markdown.js | | 13 | Important | F3 | Preview + Feature 1 interaction in PostWizard not addressed | | 15 | Important | All | No test plan specified | ### Verdict **Not Approved.** The spec needs revisions before implementation can proceed cleanly. The two critical items (8 and 9) relate to the markdown parser's correctness and security documentation. The important items (1, 2, 3, 5, 6, 10, 11, 13, 15) would each cause the implementer to stop and make judgment calls that should be design decisions. I would recommend addressing at least the critical and important items before moving to implementation.
Good review. Let me check one thing and then update the spec.

# Post Wizard News Enhancements **Date:** 2026-03-20 **Scope:** Three enhancements to the Post Wizard and News detail page ## Overview Three targeted improvements to the News post type in the Post Wizard and corresponding News detail rendering: 1. Remove the "Details" heading from news detail pages 2. Add a "Related projects" selector for news items 3. Support bold/italic markdown formatting in the body field ## Feature 1: Remove "Details" heading ### Problem When viewing a news detail page, a heading reading "Details" appears to the left of the body text. This heading adds no value and clutters the layout. ### Solution Remove the `

` element and its wrapping `
` from the body section in `NewsDetail.vue`. Collapse the two-column grid to a single-column layout for the body section, since the heading was the only content in the left column. ### Files changed - `src/components/NewsDetail.vue` -- remove heading element, simplify body section layout from `grid2` to single container ### Notes - The `subheading` field remains in the JSON schema; other post types (projects, events) still use it. - No changes to PostWizard needed for this feature. ## Feature 2: Related projects on News items ### Problem The Post Wizard's "Related" selector only shows items of the same type. For news items, editors can link to other news items but not to projects. Editors want to cross-reference relevant projects from news articles. ### Solution Add a separate `relatedProjects` field to the news JSON schema and a corresponding selector in the Post Wizard. ### JSON schema change News JSON files gain an optional field: ```json { "relatedProjects": ["urban-trees", "city-model"] } ``` This is an array of project slugs (filenames without `.json` from `content/projects/`). The existing `related` field continues to hold related news slugs. ### PostWizard changes - When `postType === 'news'`, render a second checkbox selector labeled "Related projects" below the existing "Related news items" selector. - The new selector loads options from `content/projects/index.json`, fetching each project's JSON to get the title (same pattern as the existing related loader). - New state variables: `selectedRelatedProjects`, `relatedProjectOptions`, `relatedProjectsLoading`, `relatedProjectsError`. - Independent `MAX_RELATED` (3) limit. - On draft generation, write the `relatedProjects` array into the JSON output. - On prefill (edit mode), populate `selectedRelatedProjects` from the existing JSON's `relatedProjects` field. ### NewsDetail changes - On mount, if `data.relatedProjects` exists, fetch each project's JSON from `content/projects/` in parallel (same fetch pattern as related news). - Store in a new `relatedProjects` ref. - Render a "Related projects" section with cards linking to `/projects/detail.html?slug=...`. - If both related news and related projects exist, both sections render (related posts first, then related projects). ### Files changed - `src/components/PostWizard.vue` -- new selector, state, fetch logic, draft output - `src/components/NewsDetail.vue` -- fetch and render related projects section ## Feature 3: Markdown bold/italic in body ### Problem The body field is a plain textarea rendered with `v-text`, which escapes all HTML. Editors want to format sub-headlines as bold and use italic for emphasis. ### Solution A minimal inline markdown parser that converts `**text**` to `` and `*text*` to ``, with HTML escaping to prevent injection. ### New file: `src/utils/markdown.js` Exports a single function `renderInlineMarkdown(text)` that: 1. Escapes raw HTML entities (`<`, `>`, `&`, `"`) in the input string. 2. Converts `**text**` patterns to `text`. 3. Converts `*text*` patterns to `text` (without matching inside already-converted `` pairs). 4. Returns the resulting HTML string. Processing order (escape first, then transform) ensures only our produced tags appear in the output. No injection vector exists because user-supplied HTML is escaped before markdown conversion. ### NewsDetail changes - Import `renderInlineMarkdown` from `../utils/markdown.js`. - Body paragraph rendering switches from `v-text="p"` to `v-html="renderInlineMarkdown(p)"`. - Only the body paragraphs use this; abstract/intro remains `v-text`. ### PostWizard changes - Add helper text below the body textarea: `Use **bold** and *italic* for formatting`. - The preview panel applies `renderInlineMarkdown` to body paragraphs. ### Files changed - `src/utils/markdown.js` -- new file - `src/components/NewsDetail.vue` -- import and use `renderInlineMarkdown` - `src/components/PostWizard.vue` -- helper text, preview rendering ## Security considerations - **Markdown rendering**: HTML is escaped before markdown conversion, so `v-html` only contains `` and `` tags that we produce. No user-supplied HTML passes through. - **Related projects**: Project slugs are validated with the existing `isValidSlug()` function before use in fetch URLs, preventing path traversal. ## Out of scope - Markdown in abstract/intro fields (body only for now) - Full markdown support (headers, links, lists, code blocks) - WYSIWYG/rich text editor - Related projects for post types other than news

This heading adds no value and clutters the layout. ### Solution Remove the `

` element and its wrapping `
` from the body section in `NewsDetail.vue`. The current body section uses a `grid2` two-column layout where the left column holds only the heading and the right column holds body paragraphs, gallery, and video. The target structure: - Remove the outer `
` and both child `
` wrappers. - Replace with `
` containing the body content directly (paragraphs, gallery, video). - Stop populating `item.subheading` in the data normalization for news (dead code removal). ### PostWizard preview interaction The preview panel in PostWizard also renders a subheading for the body section. When `postType === 'news'`, the preview should skip the subheading `

` and use a single-column body layout, matching the NewsDetail change. ### Files changed - `src/components/NewsDetail.vue` -- remove heading element, flatten body section from `grid2` to single `
` - `src/components/PostWizard.vue` -- preview panel: skip subheading and simplify body layout when `postType === 'news'` ### Notes - The `subheading` field remains in the JSON schema; other post types (projects, events) still use it. ## Feature 2: Related projects on News items ### Problem The Post Wizard's "Related" selector only shows items of the same type. For news items, editors can link to other news items but not to projects. Editors want to cross-reference relevant projects from news articles. ### Solution Add a separate `relatedProjects` field to the news JSON schema and a corresponding selector in the Post Wizard. ### JSON schema change News JSON files gain an optional field: ```json { "relatedProjects": ["urban-trees", "city-model"] } ``` This is an array of project slugs (filenames without `.json` from `content/projects/`). The existing `related` field continues to hold related news slugs. Existing news JSON files without `relatedProjects` are unaffected -- the code treats `undefined`/missing as an empty array. ### PostWizard changes - When `postType === 'news'`, render a second checkbox selector labeled "Related projects" below the existing "Related news items" selector. - The new selector loads options from `content/projects/index.json`, fetching each project's JSON for the title (same pattern as the existing related loader). - New state variables: `selectedRelatedProjects`, `relatedProjectOptions`, `relatedProjectsLoading`, `relatedProjectsError`. - Reuse the existing `MAX_RELATED` constant (4) for consistency with the related news selector. - On draft generation, write the `relatedProjects` array into the JSON output. - On the save path (the `if (!isEventSection(...))` block that writes `parsed.related` and `parsed.contacts`), also write or delete `parsed.relatedProjects`. - On prefill (edit mode), populate `selectedRelatedProjects` from the existing JSON's `relatedProjects` field using `cleanSlugList(data.relatedProjects, MAX_RELATED)`, matching the validation pattern used for `data.related`. ### NewsDetail changes - On mount, if `data.relatedProjects` exists, validate slugs with `isValidSlug()` and fetch each project's JSON from `content/projects/` in parallel (using `Promise.all`, same as the existing related news fetch). - Store in a new `relatedProjects` ref. - Render a "Related projects" section with `v-if="relatedProjects.length"` (conditional rendering to avoid showing an empty section). - Cards link to `/projects/detail.html?slug=...`. - If both related news and related projects exist, both sections render (related posts first, then related projects). - Also add `v-if="related.length"` to the existing "Related posts" section for consistency (currently always rendered even when empty). ### PostWizard preview The preview panel should render a second "Related projects" section for news items. Add a `loadPreviewRelatedProjects` function (mirroring `loadPreviewRelated`) that fetches from `content/projects/` and populates `previewData.relatedProjects`. The preview template renders this section with `v-if` when present and non-empty. ### Files changed - `src/components/PostWizard.vue` -- new selector, state, fetch logic, draft output, save path, prefill, preview - `src/components/NewsDetail.vue` -- fetch and render related projects section, conditional rendering on both related sections ## Feature 3: Markdown bold/italic in body ### Problem The body field is a plain textarea rendered with `v-text`, which escapes all HTML. Editors want to format sub-headlines as bold and use italic for emphasis. ### Solution A minimal inline markdown parser that converts `**text**` to `` and `*text*` to ``, with HTML escaping to prevent injection. ### New file: `src/utils/markdown.js` Exports a single function `renderInlineMarkdown(text)` that: 1. Escapes raw HTML entities (`<`, `>`, `&`, `"`) in the input string using a shared `escapeHtml` helper extracted from `sanitize.js`. 2. Converts `**text**` patterns to `text` using `/\*\*(.+?)\*\*/g`. 3. Converts `*text*` patterns to `text` using a regex that requires the `*` not be adjacent to another `*`: `/(?` and `` tags appear in the output (security). - Bold is matched before italic, so `**bold**` is not partially consumed by the italic regex. - The italic regex uses negative lookahead/lookbehind for `*` to avoid matching inside bold delimiters. **Entity interaction note**: After HTML escaping, `&` becomes `&`. A string like `**R&D**` becomes `**R&D**` after escaping, which the bold regex correctly wraps as `R&D`. The escape step must always precede markdown conversion -- reversing this order would be a security vulnerability. This is documented in code comments. **Cross-line behavior**: Markdown spans do not cross newline boundaries within a paragraph. The regex patterns use `.` (which does not match `\n` by default), so `**bold\ntext**` will not be matched. This is intentional -- single newlines within a paragraph are preserved as-is. ### Shared `escapeHtml` utility Extract the HTML escape logic from `sanitize.js`'s `allowBrText` function into a standalone `escapeHtml(input)` export. Both `allowBrText` and `renderInlineMarkdown` use `escapeHtml` as their first step, preventing duplication and divergence. `allowBrText` continues to restore `
` tags after escaping. `renderInlineMarkdown` converts markdown syntax after escaping. They are not applied to the same string. ### NewsDetail changes - Import `renderInlineMarkdown` from `../utils/markdown.js`. - Body paragraph rendering switches from `v-text="p"` to `v-html="renderInlineMarkdown(p)"`. - Only the body paragraphs use this; abstract/intro remains `v-text`. ### PostWizard changes - Add helper text below the body textarea: `Use **bold** and *italic* for formatting`. - The preview panel body paragraphs switch from `{{ paragraph }}` (text interpolation) to `v-html="renderInlineMarkdown(paragraph)"`. ### Files changed - `src/utils/sanitize.js` -- extract `escapeHtml` as a shared export; `allowBrText` reuses it - `src/utils/markdown.js` -- new file, imports `escapeHtml` from sanitize.js - `src/components/NewsDetail.vue` -- import and use `renderInlineMarkdown` for body paragraphs - `src/components/PostWizard.vue` -- helper text on body textarea, preview uses `renderInlineMarkdown` ## Security considerations - **Markdown rendering**: HTML is escaped before markdown conversion, so `v-html` only contains `` and `` tags produced by our code. No user-supplied HTML passes through. The escape-then-transform order is critical and documented in code. - **Related projects**: Project slugs are validated with the existing `isValidSlug()` function before use in fetch URLs, preventing path traversal. Prefill uses `cleanSlugList` for the same validation. ## Testing ### `src/utils/markdown.js` tests The `renderInlineMarkdown` function is a pure utility with clear contracts. Test cases: - Basic bold: `**hello**` -> `hello` - Basic italic: `*hello*` -> `hello` - Mixed: `**bold** and *italic*` -> `bold and italic` - Nested: `**bold *and italic***` -- bold wraps the whole phrase; inner `*` are literal - HTML escaping: `` -> escaped, no tags pass through - Entity interaction: `**R&D**` -> `R&D` - Unmatched asterisks: `*not closed` -> unchanged (no ``) - Empty string -> empty string - No markdown -> plain escaped text - Cross-line: `**bold\ntext**` -> not matched (no ``) - Triple asterisks: `***text***` -> `text` (or defined fallback) ### `escapeHtml` tests - Verify `<`, `>`, `&`, `"` are all escaped - Verify `allowBrText` still works after refactor (non-regression) ## Out of scope - Markdown in abstract/intro fields (body only for now) - Full markdown support (headers, links, lists, code blocks) - WYSIWYG/rich text editor - Related projects for post types other than news - Optimizing the sequential fetch pattern in PostWizard's related options loader (existing behavior, not introduced by this change)

Processing order (escape first, then transform) ensures only our produced tags appear in the output. No injection vector exists because user-supplied HTML is escaped before markdown conversion. ### NewsDetail changes - Import `renderInlineMarkdown` from `../utils/markdown.js`. - Body paragraph rendering switches from `v-text="p"` to `v-html="renderInlineMarkdown(p)"`. - Only the body paragraphs use this; abstract/intro remains `v-text`. ### PostWizard changes - Add helper text below the body textarea: `Use **bold** and *italic* for formatting`. - The preview panel applies `renderInlineMarkdown` to body paragraphs. ### Files changed - `src/utils/markdown.js` -- new file - `src/components/NewsDetail.vue` -- import and use `renderInlineMarkdown` - `src/components/PostWizard.vue` -- helper text, preview rendering ## Security considerations - **Markdown rendering**: HTML is escaped before markdown conversion, so `v-html` only contains `` and `` tags that we produce. No user-supplied HTML passes through. - **Related projects**: Project slugs are validated with the existing `isValidSlug()` function before use in fetch URLs, preventing path traversal. ## Out of scope - Markdown in abstract/intro fields (body only for now) - Full markdown support (headers, links, lists, code blocks) - WYSIWYG/rich text editor - Related projects for post types other than news

1→# Post Wizard News Enhancements 2→ 3→**Date:** 2026-03-20 4→**Scope:** Three enhancements to the Post Wizard and News detail page 5→ 6→## Overview 7→ 8→Three targeted improvements to the News post type in the Post Wizard and corresponding News detail rendering: 9→ 10→1. Remove the "Details" heading from news detail pages 11→2. Add a "Related projects" selector for news items 12→3. Support bold/italic markdown formatting in the body field 13→ 14→## Feature 1: Remove "Details" heading 15→ 16→### Problem 17→ 18→When viewing a news detail page, a heading reading "Details" appears to the left of the body text. This heading adds no value and clutters the layout. 19→ 20→### Solution 21→ 22→Remove the `

` element and its wrapping `
` from the body section in `NewsDetail.vue`. The current body section uses a `grid2` two-column layout where the left column holds only the heading and the right column holds body paragraphs, gallery, and video. The target structure: 23→ 24→- Remove the outer `
` and both child `
` wrappers. 25→- Replace with `
` containing the body content directly (paragraphs, gallery, video). 26→- Stop populating `item.subheading` in the data normalization for news (dead code removal). 27→ 28→### PostWizard preview interaction 29→ 30→The preview panel in PostWizard also renders a subheading for the body section. When `postType === 'news'`, the preview should skip the subheading `

` and use a single-column body layout, matching the NewsDetail change. 31→ 32→### Files changed 33→ 34→- `src/components/NewsDetail.vue` -- remove heading element, flatten body section from `grid2` to single `
` 35→- `src/components/PostWizard.vue` -- preview panel: skip subheading and simplify body layout when `postType === 'news'` 36→ 37→### Notes 38→ 39→- The `subheading` field remains in the JSON schema; other post types (projects, events) still use it. 40→ 41→## Feature 2: Related projects on News items 42→ 43→### Problem 44→ 45→The Post Wizard's "Related" selector only shows items of the same type. For news items, editors can link to other news items but not to projects. Editors want to cross-reference relevant projects from news articles. 46→ 47→### Solution 48→ 49→Add a separate `relatedProjects` field to the news JSON schema and a corresponding selector in the Post Wizard. 50→ 51→### JSON schema change 52→ 53→News JSON files gain an optional field: 54→ 55→```json 56→{ 57→ "relatedProjects": ["urban-trees", "city-model"] 58→} 59→``` 60→ 61→This is an array of project slugs (filenames without `.json` from `content/projects/`). The existing `related` field continues to hold related news slugs. 62→ 63→Existing news JSON files without `relatedProjects` are unaffected -- the code treats `undefined`/missing as an empty array. 64→ 65→### PostWizard changes 66→ 67→- When `postType === 'news'`, render a second checkbox selector labeled "Related projects" below the existing "Related news items" selector. 68→- The new selector loads options from `content/projects/index.json`, fetching each project's JSON for the title (same pattern as the existing related loader). 69→- New state variables: `selectedRelatedProjects`, `relatedProjectOptions`, `relatedProjectsLoading`, `relatedProjectsError`. 70→- Reuse the existing `MAX_RELATED` constant (4) for consistency with the related news selector. 71→- On draft generation, write the `relatedProjects` array into the JSON output. 72→- On the save path (the `if (!isEventSection(...))` block that writes `parsed.related` and `parsed.contacts`), also write or delete `parsed.relatedProjects`. 73→- On prefill (edit mode), populate `selectedRelatedProjects` from the existing JSON's `relatedProjects` field using `cleanSlugList(data.relatedProjects, MAX_RELATED)`, matching the validation pattern used for `data.related`. 74→ 75→### NewsDetail changes 76→ 77→- On mount, if `data.relatedProjects` exists, validate slugs with `isValidSlug()` and fetch each project's JSON from `content/projects/` in parallel (using `Promise.all`, same as the existing related news fetch). 78→- Store in a new `relatedProjects` ref. 79→- Render a "Related projects" section with `v-if="relatedProjects.length"` (conditional rendering to avoid showing an empty section). 80→- Cards link to `/projects/detail.html?slug=...`. 81→- If both related news and related projects exist, both sections render (related posts first, then related projects). 82→- Also add `v-if="related.length"` to the existing "Related posts" section for consistency (currently always rendered even when empty). 83→ 84→### PostWizard preview 85→ 86→The preview panel should render a second "Related projects" section for news items. Add a `loadPreviewRelatedProjects` function (mirroring `loadPreviewRelated`) that fetches from `content/projects/` and populates `previewData.relatedProjects`. The preview template renders this section with `v-if` when present and non-empty. 87→ 88→### Files changed 89→ 90→- `src/components/PostWizard.vue` -- new selector, state, fetch logic, draft output, save path, prefill, preview 91→- `src/components/NewsDetail.vue` -- fetch and render related projects section, conditional rendering on both related sections 92→ 93→## Feature 3: Markdown bold/italic in body 94→ 95→### Problem 96→ 97→The body field is a plain textarea rendered with `v-text`, which escapes all HTML. Editors want to format sub-headlines as bold and use italic for emphasis. 98→ 99→### Solution 100→ 101→A minimal inline markdown parser that converts `**text**` to `` and `*text*` to ``, with HTML escaping to prevent injection. 102→ 103→### New file: `src/utils/markdown.js` 104→ 105→Exports a single function `renderInlineMarkdown(text)` that: 106→ 107→1. Escapes raw HTML entities (`<`, `>`, `&`, `"`) in the input string using a shared `escapeHtml` helper extracted from `sanitize.js`. 108→2. Converts `**text**` patterns to `text` using `/\*\*(.+?)\*\*/g`. 109→3. Converts `*text*` patterns to `text` using a regex that requires the `*` not be adjacent to another `*`: `/(?` and `` tags appear in the output (security). 114→- Bold is matched before italic, so `**bold**` is not partially consumed by the italic regex. 115→- The italic regex uses negative lookahead/lookbehind for `*` to avoid matching inside bold delimiters. 116→ 117→**Entity interaction note**: After HTML escaping, `&` becomes `&`. A string like `**R&D**` becomes `**R&D**` after escaping, which the bold regex correctly wraps as `R&D`. The escape step must always precede markdown conversion -- reversing this order would be a security vulnerability. This is documented in code comments. 118→ 119→**Cross-line behavior**: Markdown spans do not cross newline boundaries within a paragraph. The regex patterns use `.` (which does not match `\n` by default), so `**bold\ntext**` will not be matched. This is intentional -- single newlines within a paragraph are preserved as-is. 120→ 121→### Shared `escapeHtml` utility 122→ 123→Extract the HTML escape logic from `sanitize.js`'s `allowBrText` function into a standalone `escapeHtml(input)` export. Both `allowBrText` and `renderInlineMarkdown` use `escapeHtml` as their first step, preventing duplication and divergence. `allowBrText` continues to restore `
` tags after escaping. `renderInlineMarkdown` converts markdown syntax after escaping. They are not applied to the same string. 124→ 125→### NewsDetail changes 126→ 127→- Import `renderInlineMarkdown` from `../utils/markdown.js`. 128→- Body paragraph rendering switches from `v-text="p"` to `v-html="renderInlineMarkdown(p)"`. 129→- Only the body paragraphs use this; abstract/intro remains `v-text`. 130→ 131→### PostWizard changes 132→ 133→- Add helper text below the body textarea: `Use **bold** and *italic* for formatting`. 134→- The preview panel body paragraphs switch from `{{ paragraph }}` (text interpolation) to `v-html="renderInlineMarkdown(paragraph)"`. 135→ 136→### Files changed 137→ 138→- `src/utils/sanitize.js` -- extract `escapeHtml` as a shared export; `allowBrText` reuses it 139→- `src/utils/markdown.js` -- new file, imports `escapeHtml` from sanitize.js 140→- `src/components/NewsDetail.vue` -- import and use `renderInlineMarkdown` for body paragraphs 141→- `src/components/PostWizard.vue` -- helper text on body textarea, preview uses `renderInlineMarkdown` 142→ 143→## Security considerations 144→ 145→- **Markdown rendering**: HTML is escaped before markdown conversion, so `v-html` only contains `` and `` tags produced by our code. No user-supplied HTML passes through. The escape-then-transform order is critical and documented in code. 146→- **Related projects**: Project slugs are validated with the existing `isValidSlug()` function before use in fetch URLs, preventing path traversal. Prefill uses `cleanSlugList` for the same validation. 147→ 148→## Testing 149→ 150→### `src/utils/markdown.js` tests 151→ 152→The `renderInlineMarkdown` function is a pure utility with clear contracts. Test cases: 153→ 154→- Basic bold: `**hello**` -> `hello` 155→- Basic italic: `*hello*` -> `hello` 156→- Mixed: `**bold** and *italic*` -> `bold and italic` 157→- Nested: `**bold *and italic***` -- bold wraps the whole phrase; inner `*` are literal 158→- HTML escaping: `` -> escaped, no tags pass through 159→- Entity interaction: `**R&D**` -> `R&D` 160→- Unmatched asterisks: `*not closed` -> unchanged (no ``) 161→- Empty string -> empty string 162→- No markdown -> plain escaped text 163→- Cross-line: `**bold\ntext**` -> not matched (no ``) 164→- Triple asterisks: `***text***` -> `text` (or defined fallback) 165→ 166→### `escapeHtml` tests 167→ 168→- Verify `<`, `>`, `&`, `"` are all escaped 169→- Verify `allowBrText` still works after refactor (non-regression) 170→ 171→## Out of scope 172→ 173→- Markdown in abstract/intro fields (body only for now) 174→- Full markdown support (headers, links, lists, code blocks) 175→- WYSIWYG/rich text editor 176→- Related projects for post types other than news 177→- Optimizing the sequential fetch pattern in PostWizard's related options loader (existing behavior, not introduced by this change) 178→

1→/** Basic URL sanitizer to avoid javascript: and other dangerous schemes. */ 2→export function sanitizeUrl(input) { 3→ if (typeof input !== 'string' || !input.trim()) return '#' 4→ const url = input.trim() 5→ // Allow relative URLs 6→ if (url.startsWith('/') || url.startsWith('./') || url.startsWith('../')) return url 7→ try { 8→ const u = new URL(url) 9→ const allowed = ['http:', 'https:'] // Reject data:, blob:, javascript:, etc. 10→ if (!allowed.includes(u.protocol)) return '#' 11→ return u.toString() 12→ } catch { 13→ return '#' 14→ } 15→} 16→ 17→/** Sanitize image/video src: allow only http(s) or relative. */ 18→export function sanitizeSrc(input) { 19→ if (typeof input !== 'string' || !input.trim()) return null 20→ const url = input.trim() 21→ if (url.startsWith('/') || url.startsWith('./') || url.startsWith('../')) return url 22→ try { 23→ const u = new URL(url) 24→ const allowed = ['http:', 'https:'] 25→ if (!allowed.includes(u.protocol)) return null 26→ return u.toString() 27→ } catch { 28→ return null 29→ } 30→} 31→ 32→/** Escape all HTML then permit
tags back (simple allowlist). */ 33→export function allowBrText(input) { 34→ if (typeof input !== 'string') return '' 35→ const escaped = input 36→ .replace(/&/g, '&') 37→ .replace(//g, '>') 39→ // Restore line breaks represented as
or
in the source 40→ return escaped 41→ .replace(/<br\s*\/?>/gi, '

I now have a comprehensive understanding of the codebase and the spec. Let me compile the review. --- ## Design Spec Review -- Second Pass **Spec:** `/Users/vasnas/scratch/dtcc-web-1/docs/superpowers/specs/2026-03-20-post-wizard-news-enhancements-design.md` ### Resolution of Previously Identified Issues I have verified each of the 15 previously raised issues against the updated spec and the current codebase. **Issue 8 (Critical -- Bold vs italic regex interaction):** Resolved. The spec now provides exact regex patterns at lines 108-109 and explicitly states the processing order (escape, then bold, then italic) at lines 112-115. The italic regex uses negative lookahead/lookbehind to avoid consuming bold delimiters. This is sufficient for implementation. **Issue 9 (Critical -- Escape-then-transform entity interaction):** Resolved. Lines 117 spells out the `**R&D**` -> `**R&D**` -> `R&D` pipeline with a clear security rationale. The test plan at line 159 includes a specific test case for this. **Issue 1 (Important -- Target DOM structure):** Resolved. Lines 24-25 specify removing `grid2` and flattening to `
`. **Issue 2 (Important -- MAX_RELATED):** Resolved. Line 70 explicitly states "Reuse the existing `MAX_RELATED` constant (4)". **Issue 3 (Important -- Prefill validation):** Resolved. Line 73 specifies `cleanSlugList(data.relatedProjects, MAX_RELATED)` matching the existing pattern at PostWizard.vue line 887. **Issue 5 (Important -- Preview panel):** Resolved. Lines 30-35 address preview subheading removal for Feature 1. Lines 86-87 address the preview for Feature 2's related projects. **Issue 6 (Important -- Save path):** Resolved. Line 72 explicitly names the save path block ("the `if (!isEventSection(...))` block") and what to add. **Issue 10 (Important -- Cross-line markdown):** Resolved. Lines 119 states the intended behavior and the mechanism (`.` not matching `\n`). Test case at line 163 covers it. **Issue 11 (Important -- allowBrText relationship):** Resolved. Lines 107-108 and 121-123 specify extracting `escapeHtml` from `sanitize.js` as a shared export, with both `allowBrText` and `renderInlineMarkdown` consuming it. **Issue 13 (Important -- Preview + Feature 1 interaction):** Resolved. Lines 30-35 cover this directly. **Issue 15 (Important -- Test plan):** Resolved. Lines 150-169 include a comprehensive test plan for both `renderInlineMarkdown` and `escapeHtml`. ### New Issues Found in Updated Spec **Issue A (Important): Missing `isValidSlug` filtering on existing related news slugs in NewsDetail creates an inconsistency with the spec's stated approach.** The spec at line 77 says: "if `data.relatedProjects` exists, validate slugs with `isValidSlug()` and fetch each project's JSON." However, the existing related news fetch in `NewsDetail.vue` (lines 228-248) does NOT validate individual slugs with `isValidSlug()` before building fetch URLs. The spec should either: - Apply the same `isValidSlug` filter to `relatedProjects` only (accepting the existing inconsistency), or - Note that the existing related-news fetch should also be hardened as a separate cleanup item. This matters because without `isValidSlug`, a malicious slug in the JSON could craft a path traversal in the fetch URL. The spec currently implies the new code is following an existing pattern, but it is actually introducing a stricter pattern only for projects. The implementer needs to know whether to also fix the existing code or just match the spec's stated approach. Recommendation: Add a one-line note acknowledging the inconsistency, e.g., "The existing related-news fetch does not validate slugs; the new relatedProjects fetch should apply `isValidSlug()` regardless. Hardening the existing related-news fetch is out of scope." **Issue B (Suggestion): Triple-asterisk behavior is undefined in the spec body, only in the test plan.** Line 164 includes: `***text***` -> `text` (or defined fallback). The "or defined fallback" phrasing leaves the implementer with a judgment call. Given the specified regex processing order (bold first via `/\*\*(.+?)\*\*/g`, then italic), the actual behavior would be: `***text***` matches bold as `*text*`, then the italic pass converts the inner `*text*` to `text`. This is the natural consequence of the spec's stated rules and should work correctly, but the test plan should drop the "or defined fallback" hedge and just state the expected output since it is deterministic from the regex order. **Issue C (Suggestion): The related projects section ordering relative to papers is unspecified in NewsDetail.** Line 81 says: "If both related news and related projects exist, both sections render (related posts first, then related projects)." The current template has the ordering: related posts -> papers. The spec does not state whether related projects should appear before or after the papers section. Looking at the template structure, the natural insertion point would be between related posts and papers, making the final order: related posts -> related projects -> papers. The spec should confirm this ordering. **Issue D (Suggestion): The `detailHref` function for related projects cards needs a different base path.** Line 80 says cards link to `/projects/detail.html?slug=...`. The existing `detailHref` function in NewsDetail.vue (line 146) hardcodes the news path: `withBase('news/detail.html?slug=...')`. The implementer will need a second helper or a parameterized version for the projects path. This is straightforward but worth noting to avoid confusion -- the spec correctly states the target URL but does not mention that a new or modified helper is needed. ### Items That Are Clean The following aspects of the spec are well-specified and ready for implementation without ambiguity: - Feature 1's DOM flattening (lines 22-26) precisely matches the structure at NewsDetail.vue lines 25-60. - Feature 1's subheading dead-code removal (line 26) correctly identifies that the news draft payload at PostWizard.vue line 1803-1820 already omits `subheading` for news, so the dead code is in NewsDetail.vue line 219 (`subheading: data.subheading || data.headline || 'Details'`). - Feature 2's state variables (line 69) follow the exact naming pattern from the existing related selector. - Feature 2's save path description (line 72) correctly identifies the block at PostWizard.vue lines 2104-2118. - Feature 3's `escapeHtml` extraction from `sanitize.js` (lines 107-108, 121-123) is clear -- the escape logic is at sanitize.js lines 35-37. - Feature 3's security model (lines 145-146) is sound. - The test plan (lines 150-169) covers the critical cases. - The out-of-scope section (lines 173-177) is well-bounded. ### Verdict The spec has substantially improved. Both previous critical issues are fully resolved. All but one of the previous important issues are cleanly resolved. The remaining items are: - **1 Important issue** (Issue A): The `isValidSlug` inconsistency needs a one-line clarification. - **3 Suggestions** (Issues B, C, D): Minor clarifications that would make implementation cleaner but are not blockers. **Conditional Approval.** If Issue A is addressed with a brief clarifying note, the spec is ready for implementation. The three suggestions are optional polish.

file.content:
### NewsDetail changes

- On mount, if `data.relatedProjects` exists, validate slugs with `isValidSlug()` and fetch each project's JSON from `content/projects/` in parallel (using `Promise.all`, same as the existing related news fetch).
- Store in a new `relatedProjects` ref.
- Render a "Related projects" section with `v-if="relatedProjects.length"` (conditional rendering to avoid showing an empty section).
- Cards link to `/projects/detail.html?slug=...`.
- If both related news and related projects exist, both sections render (related posts first, then related projects).
- Also add `v-if="related.length"` to the existing "Related posts" section for consistency (currently always rendered even when empty).

### PostWizard preview

The preview panel should render a second "Related projects" section for news items. Add a `loadPreviewRelatedProjects` function (mirroring `loadPreviewRelated`) that fetches from `content/projects/` and populates `previewData.relatedProjects`. The preview template renders this section with `v-if` when present and non-empty.

### Files changed

- `src/components/PostWizard.vue` -- new selector, state, fetch logic, draft output, save path, prefill, preview
- `src/components/NewsDetail.vue` -- fetch and render related projects section, conditional rendering on both related sections

## Feature 3: Markdown bold/italic in body
